verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type I TM protein resident in the ER membrane, with its N-terminal EF-hand/SAM domain in the ER lumen. Upon ER Ca2+ store depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes and translocates to ER-PM junctions where it gates Orai channels. A documented minority pool also reaches the plasma membrane itself (surface STIM1), with the former ER-luminal domain exposed extracellularly, and antibodies targeting this surface pool have been reported. Baseline is ER-resident, but a real PM fraction exists.
